## Summary

This PR addresses two code issues and adds missing test coverage for edge cases in core utility functions.

## Changes

### Bug Fixes

- **src/security/audit-channel.ts**: Skip accounts with unresolved SecretRef in collectChannelSecurityFindings. Previously openclaw status would crash with an unhandled error when channels.telegram.botToken (or any channel credential) was stored as a file-backed SecretRef, because plugin.config.resolveAccount throws for unresolved refs. Now those accounts are skipped with a continue so the rest of the audit proceeds normally. Closes #36737.

- **src/infra/format-time/format-duration.ts**:  ormatDurationPrecise now returns 'unknown' for negative input (consistent with  ormatDurationHuman). Previously it returned e.g. '-500ms' for -500.

### Test Coverage

-  ormat-time.test.ts: add  ormatDurationPrecise negative tests,  ormatDurationSeconds negative clamping test,  ormatDurationHuman zero-input test,  ormatTimeAgo NaN/Infinity tests,  ormatRelativeTimestamp NaN/Infinity tests
- cli-utils.test.ts: add parseDurationMs tests for defaultUnit option (previously uncovered), zero-value inputs ('0', '0ms', '0s'), and invalid single-token inputs ('abc', '   ', '')
title: Fix SecretRef crash in audit and add edge-case test coverage

## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: `parseConfigPath` naively used `.split(".")`, causing custom model provider keys containing periods (like `llama.cpp`) to be shattered into nested objects.
- Why it matters: Users attempting to set local AI endpoints via CLI (`/config set models.providers.llama.cpp.baseUrl ...`) face silent failures because the configuration never reaches the correct `models.providers["llama.cpp"]` key.
- What changed: Replaced the brittle `.split(".")` with a robust state-machine parser in `config-paths.ts`. It now fully supports single/double-quoted bracket notation (e.g., `models.providers["llama.cpp"].baseUrl`).
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Existing valid dot-notation config paths behave exactly the same. No external dependencies were added.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #36871

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users can now correctly configure paths with special characters by using bracket notation. 
Example CLI usage that now works: 
`/config set 'models.providers["llama.cpp"].baseUrl' http://127.0.0.1:8080`

Added a `<Tip>` block in `docs/concepts/models.md` and `docs/zh-CN/concepts/models.md` to warn users about this syntax requirement.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (Yes/No) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (Yes/No) No
- New/changed network calls? (Yes/No) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (Yes/No) No
- Data access scope changed? (Yes/No) No
- If any Yes, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: All
- Runtime/container: Node.js 22
- Model/provider: Custom providers containing periods (e.g. `llama.cpp`)

### Steps

1. Execute: `openclaw config set 'models.providers["llama.cpp"].baseUrl' http://localhost`
2. Check parsed configuration using `openclaw config get models.providers`

### Expected

The provider key correctly resolves as `"llama.cpp"`.

### Actual (before fix)

The config structure broke into `{"llama": {"cpp": ...}}` returning `undefined` for `models.providers["llama.cpp"]`.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets: 
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Evaluated edge cases like empty brackets, adjacent dots (`foo..bar`), and mismatched quotes. All are properly rejected.
- Edge cases checked: Escaped quotes inside brackets and trailing periods are handled accurately.
- What you did **not** verify: N/A

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (Yes/No) Yes
- Config/env changes? (Yes/No) No
- Migration needed? (Yes/No) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert `src/config/config-paths.ts`.
- Files/config to restore: `src/config/config-paths.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: Unexpected configuration loading failures during startup.

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: Edge-case string parsing regressions.
  - Mitigation: Added extensive unit test coverage in `config-misc.test.ts` testing nested structures, invalid spaces, array properties, and object bracket accesses.
title: fix(config): support bracket notation in paths for keys with periods

body:
## Summary

Adds group/channel messaging support to the Synology Chat plugin alongside existing DM support.

Ref #23464 — this is a clean reimplementation on current `main`, not a rebase of the closed PR.

### Architecture

- **Token-based detection**: bot token = DM, channel outgoing webhook token = group. No heuristics.
- **Session keys**: DM = `synology-chat-{userId}`, Group = `synology-chat:group:{channelId}`
- **Reply routing**: DM replies → bot incoming webhook with `user_ids`. Group replies → channel-specific incoming webhook.
- **Access control**: `groupPolicy` (`disabled`/`open`/`allowlist`) + `groupAllowFrom` for group channels, independent from `dmPolicy`.

### Changes (9 files)

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `types.ts` | `GroupAccessPolicy` type, channel webhook config interfaces |
| `accounts.ts` | `parseChannelTokensFromEnv()`, `parseChannelWebhooksFromEnv()`, group config resolution |
| `client.ts` | `sendWithRetry()` refactor, new `sendToChannel()` for channel replies |
| `webhook-handler.ts` | `matchToken()` multi-token matching, group policy enforcement, channel-aware sessions/routing |
| `channel.ts` | `chatTypes: ["direct","group"]`, deliver callback routes to channel webhooks, group warnings |
| `accounts.test.ts` | 6 new tests (env parsing, group config resolution) |
| `webhook-handler.test.ts` | 6 new tests (group routing, policy, allowlist, sendToChannel, DM coexistence) |
| `channel.test.ts` | 3 new tests (capabilities, group warnings) |
| `docs/channels/synology-chat.md` | Full rewrite: DM + group setup, config reference, env vars, troubleshooting |

### Key design decisions

- Group messaging is **disabled by default** (`groupPolicy: "disabled"`) — opt-in only
- `channelTokens` and `channelWebhooks` are keyed by Synology channel ID
- Env vars `SYNOLOGY_CHANNEL_TOKEN_<id>` / `SYNOLOGY_CHANNEL_WEBHOOK_<id>` supported
- Channel-only setup works (no bot token needed if only doing group messaging)
- All existing DM behavior is preserved — zero regressions

### Tests

- **98 tests passing**, zero regressions
- 15 new tests covering group routing, access control, env parsing, warnings, and DM coexistence

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm vitest run extensions/synology-chat/` — 98/98 passing
- [ ] Integration test on real Synology NAS: DM + channel coexistence (planned before merge)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: feat(synology-chat): add group/channel support via outgoing + incoming webhooks

body:
## Problem

After upgrading to 2026.3.2, users with multi-channel setups (WebChat/TUI + iMessage) experienced cross-channel reply leakage. WebChat replies were also delivered to iMessage contacts because `dmScope` defaulted to `"main"` when not explicitly configured.

## Root Cause

Five runtime sites defaulted `dmScope` to `"main"` via `?? "main"`, while onboarding correctly defaults new installs to `"per-channel-peer"`. After an upgrade that didn't preserve the explicit dmScope setting, the runtime silently fell back to the unsafe `"main"` default, causing all channels to share the same session.

## Fix

- Introduce a shared `DEFAULT_DM_SCOPE` constant (`"per-channel-peer"`) in `config/types.base.ts`
- Replace all 5 hardcoded `?? "main"` fallbacks with the shared constant
- Consolidate with the onboarding default (was a separate `ONBOARDING_DEFAULT_DM_SCOPE` constant)

### Files changed (8):
- `src/config/types.base.ts` — new `DEFAULT_DM_SCOPE` constant
- `src/infra/outbound/outbound-session.ts` — outbound routing
- `src/routing/resolve-route.ts` — inbound routing  
- `src/routing/session-key.ts` — session key generation
- `src/security/audit-channel.ts` — security audit
- `src/commands/doctor-security.ts` — doctor checks
- `src/commands/onboard-config.ts` — consolidated with shared constant
- `src/commands/onboard-config.test.ts` — updated imports

## Testing

- All 96 existing tests pass (onboard-config: 3, session-key: 14, audit: 79)
- TypeScript compilation clean
- No behavior change for users who explicitly set `session.dmScope` in config

## Breaking Change Note

Users who intentionally relied on the implicit `"main"` default (without setting it in config) will now get `"per-channel-peer"` behavior. This is the safer default and matches what onboarding has always set for new installs. Users who want `"main"` can set it explicitly: `openclaw config set session.dmScope main`

Fixes #36687
title: fix: use safe dmScope default to prevent cross-channel reply leakage

body:
## Summary
- memoize `resolvePluginSdkAliasFile` results by source/dist/modulePath+env
- avoid repeating the same synchronous `fs.existsSync` directory walk when plugin-sdk aliases are resolved multiple times
- expose a test-only cache reset helper and add a focused test proving repeated lookups are cache hits

## Why
Plugin loading resolves many plugin-sdk alias entries. Without memoization, each lookup repeats the same sync filesystem checks. This change keeps behavior the same while reducing startup sync I/O in that startup path.

## Risk
Low. Resolution semantics are unchanged; only repeated calls are cached. Existing alias preference tests remain, and a new memoization test verifies cache behavior.
title: perf(plugins): memoize plugin-sdk alias resolution

(`No`) - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`) (reads existing access token from ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json) - New/changed network calls? (`Yes`) — script makes Matrix Client-Server API calls (sync, keys/query, sendToDevice) - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`) - Data access scope changed? No new credentials are created or stored. From Element (or another Matrix client), open the bot device info and choose "Verify" 4. Confirm the emoji match in the terminal ### Expected - Verification completes, bot device is marked as verified ### Actual - Verification completes successfully ## Evidence - [x] Tested manually against a Matrix homeserver with Element as the verifying client ## Human Verification (required) I ran the script to verify my bot's device. Now I can message my openclaw bot with a verified check: I did NOT verify multi-account setups orhomeservers other than matrix.org. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`Yes`) - Config/env changes? (`No`) - Migration needed? (`No`) - If yes, exact upgrade steps: ## Failure Recovery (if this breaks) - How to disable/revert this change quickly: Delete `scripts/matrix_verify.py` - Files/config to restore: None - Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: None — standalone script, no impact on existing code ## Risks and Mitigations List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`. - Risk: Does not address cross-signing key bootstrap (#21023), only device-to-device verification - Mitigation: Documented as a known limitation; a proper fix belongs in the Matrix plugin 🤖 Co-written with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: scripts: add Matrix device verification workaround

body:
## Summary

- ACP dispatch (`tryDispatchAcpReply`) calls `acpManager.runTurn()` without an abort signal, so a stalled upstream SSE stream blocks the session queue forever
- Wire `resolveAgentTimeoutMs()` (respects `agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds`) through the existing `withTimeout` utility to abort hung turns
- Remove redundant `async/await` wrapper on `onEvent` callback (eliminates unnecessary intermediate promise per SSE event)

## Context

The embedded PI runner already has timeout protection via `setTimeout` → `abortRun()` in `pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts`. The ACP runtime's `runTurn()` accepts an optional `signal` parameter and correctly wires it through to `AbortSignal.any()`, but the ACP dispatch caller never provided one.

When the Anthropic API streaming connection silently stalls (TCP stays open, no SSE events), the `for await` in `runTurn()` blocks indefinitely. The `KeyedAsyncQueue` promise chain blocks all subsequent messages for that session key. The diagnostic system warns (`logSessionStuck`) but never recovers.

Refs #17258

## Test plan

- [x] New test: verifies abort signal is passed to `runTurn`
- [x] New test: verifies stalled `runTurn` is aborted when timeout fires (fake timers)
- [x] Existing tests pass (`pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` — 9/9)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: fix(acp): pass abort signal to runTurn to prevent stuck sessions

## Summary

Fixes #35881

## Problem

Cron-related files were being created with default `0644` permissions (world-readable), and the `runs/` directory with `0755`. While the parent `~/.openclaw/cron/` directory is `0700` (providing protection in practice), creating files with world-readable permissions is not defense-in-depth and may expose sensitive cron job data (schedules, message content, delivery targets) in environments where the home directory or umask is configured differently.

## Changes

### `src/cron/store.ts`
- `mkdir` for cron directory: added `mode: 0o700`
- `writeFile` for temp file: changed from `"utf-8"` to `{ encoding: "utf-8", mode: 0o600 }`
- After `copyFile` to `.bak`: added `chmod(..., 0o600)` (best-effort, same try/catch block)

### `src/cron/run-log.ts`
- `mkdir` for `runs/` directory: added `mode: 0o700`
- `appendFile` for log entries: changed from `"utf-8"` to `{ encoding: "utf-8", mode: 0o600 }`
- `writeFile` in `pruneIfNeeded`: changed from `"utf-8"` to `{ encoding: "utf-8", mode: 0o600 }`

## Tests Added

- `store.test.ts`: 3 new tests verifying `0700` dir and `0600` file/bak permissions
- `run-log.test.ts`: 2 new tests verifying `0700` runs dir and `0600` log file permissions
title: fix(cron): create cron files and directories with restrictive permissions

body:
## Summary

Fixes #36439 — long messages sent through the Synology Chat plugin are silently truncated by the Synology Chat API beyond ~2000 characters.

### Root cause

`sendMessage()` in `client.ts` sends the full text in a single HTTP payload. The Synology Chat API silently truncates at ~2000 chars — no error, no warning, just lost content.

The existing `textChunkLimit: 2000` in the channel's `outbound` config only applies to the `sendText` path (API-initiated sends), **not** to the webhook reply dispatcher path which calls `sendMessage()` directly.

### Fix

- Added `splitTextForSynology(text, limit)` — splits long text into chunks of 1800 chars max (safety margin below the ~2000 API limit), preferring newline > space > hard-cut boundaries
- Refactored `sendMessage()` to iterate over chunks, each sent with full retry logic
- Returns `false` immediately if any chunk fails (no partial delivery without error)
- Exported `SYNOLOGY_CHUNK_LIMIT` and `splitTextForSynology` for testability

### Why `client.ts` and not `webhook-handler.ts`?

The reply path goes: `channel.ts` → `dispatchReplyWithBufferedBlockDispatcher` → `deliver()` callback → `sendMessage()`. The deliver callback in `channel.ts` returns `null`, so the `if (reply)` check in `webhook-handler.ts` is never reached. Chunking must happen inside `sendMessage()` itself, at the HTTP transport layer.

### Tests

- 8 new unit tests for `splitTextForSynology` (short text, exact limit, multi-chunk join, newline split, space split, hard cut, custom limit)
- 3 new tests for `sendMessage` chunking (multi-request for long text, single request for short text, failure propagation)
- Full suite: **94 tests passing**, zero regressions

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm vitest run extensions/synology-chat/` — 94/94 passing
- [ ] Integration test on real Synology NAS (planned before merge)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: fix(synology-chat): chunk long messages to prevent silent truncation

body:
## Summary

Fixes #32123 — binary files (xlsx, pdf, etc.) sent via the `message` tool `filePath` parameter were corrupted when delivered through the Feishu channel.

## Root Cause

When `sendMediaFeishu` handles a local file path, it was:
1. Reading the file into a Buffer via `loadWebMedia` → `readLocalFileSafely`
2. Passing the Buffer to `uploadFileFeishu` → Lark SDK `client.im.file.create()`
3. The Lark SDK uses axios with `Content-Type: multipart/form-data`, and axios's form-data serialization layer can corrupt raw Buffers during multipart boundary encoding

The issue reporter confirmed that direct SDK uploads with `fs.createReadStream()` work fine, while the same file through the OpenClaw pipeline gets corrupted.

## Fix

For local file paths, we now:
1. Still call `loadWebMedia` for **path validation and size checking** (security/SSRF guards)
2. Pass the **resolved file path string** (not the buffer) to `uploadFileFeishu`/`uploadImageFeishu`
3. These functions already handle string paths by creating `fs.createReadStream()`, which the form-data library serializes correctly

For remote URLs and direct buffer inputs, the existing buffer-based path is preserved unchanged.

## Tests

Added 4 new test cases:
- ✅ Local file uploads use ReadStream (not Buffer)
- ✅ Local image uploads use ReadStream (not Buffer)
- ✅ Remote URLs still use Buffer (existing behavior)
- ✅ Direct `mediaBuffer` still uses Buffer (existing behavior)

## Related

- #32123 — original bug report with detailed investigation
- Lark SDK issue: https://github.com/larksuite/node-sdk/issues/121 (Buffer vs ReadStream)
title: fix(feishu): use ReadStream for local file uploads to prevent binary corruption

## Summary

- **Problem:** Anthropic Claude Max plan surfaces temporary usage/rate-limit failures as HTTP 402 instead of 429. OpenClaw unconditionally maps all 402s to `billing`, producing a misleading "run out of credits" warning for users with valid billing.
- **Fix:** Before defaulting to `billing`, check the 402 payload for explicit rate-limit signals (`isRateLimitErrorMessage`). This follows the same pattern as the HTTP 400 billing check merged in #36783.

body:
## Summary

Fixes #36511 — V8 fatal crash when running `openclaw models auth login --provider openai-codex` on Linux.

**Root cause:** The `models auth login` command resolves providers exclusively from installed plugins. No plugin registers `openai-codex`, so when `--provider openai-codex` is passed the command reaches a code path inside `resolvePluginProviders` / the IC resolution loop that triggers a V8 fatal error (`Runtime_LoadIC_Miss` bounds-check failure) in the compiled `openclaw-models` binary on Linux.

**Fix:** Register `openai-codex` as a built-in provider in the `models auth login` flow, reusing the existing `loginOpenAICodexOAuth` function (already used for onboarding). Built-in providers are merged ahead of plugin-registered ones; if a plugin later registers the same `openai-codex` id, the plugin wins.

## Changes

- **`src/commands/models/auth.builtin-providers.ts`** — New file: `BUILTIN_AUTH_PROVIDERS` array containing an `openai-codex` provider plugin that delegates to `loginOpenAICodexOAuth` and returns a `ProviderAuthResult` via `buildOauthProviderAuthResult`.
- **`src/commands/models/auth.ts`** — `modelsAuthLoginCommand` now merges built-in providers with plugin providers before any lookup or selection prompt, so `--provider openai-codex` resolves correctly without a plugin installed.
- **`src/commands/models/auth.builtin-providers.test.ts`** — Unit tests covering successful OAuth, null/cancelled OAuth, and error propagation.

## Test plan

- [ ] `pnpm test src/commands/models/auth.builtin-providers.test.ts` — 4 new tests pass
- [ ] `pnpm test src/commands/openai-codex-oauth.test.ts` — existing tests still pass
- [ ] Manual: `openclaw models auth login --provider openai-codex` launches OAuth flow without crashing

— [Joel Nishanth](https://offlyn.ai) · [offlyn.AI](https://offlyn.ai)

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
title: fix(models): add openai-codex as built-in provider for models auth login

body:
## Summary

Fixes #36697

In Telegram DMs with `channels.telegram.streaming: "partial"`, `materialize()` was called at the end of a streamed reply. The sequence was:

1. `stop()` → `flush()` → `sendMessageDraft(chatId, draftId, finalText)` — updates the draft in Telegram's input area
2. `sendMessage(chatId, finalText)` — creates the real message in chat history

Between steps 1 and 2, Telegram briefly shows **both** the draft (in the input area) and the newly delivered real message, giving the impression of a duplicate reply. Reopening the chat caused Telegram to clear the stale draft, hiding the duplicate.

**Fix**: After `sendMessage` succeeds in `materialize()`, call `sendMessageDraft(chatId, draftId, "")` to explicitly clear the draft. This removes the stale input-area copy immediately after the real message is delivered.

## Changes

- `src/telegram/draft-stream.ts`: In `materialize()`, after a successful `sendMessage`, call `resolvedDraftApi` with an empty string to clear the Telegram draft. Wrapped in try/catch — clearing is best-effort and failure is cosmetic.
- `src/telegram/draft-stream.test.ts`: Added test `"clears draft after materializing to avoid duplicate display in DM"` verifying the empty-string `sendMessageDraft` call is made with the correct `chatId` and thread params.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk**: `sendMessageDraft` with empty string might fail on some Telegram server versions.
  - **Mitigation**: The call is fully wrapped in try/catch; if it fails, the behavior degrades to the current state (draft lingers until chat reopen).
- **Risk**: Clearing the draft using the wrong ID after a stream rotation.
  - **Mitigation**: `streamDraftId` is captured at the point of the successful `sendMessage`, so the correct draft ID is always used.

— [Joel Nishanth](https://offlyn.ai) · [offlyn.AI](https://offlyn.ai)
title: fix(telegram): clear DM draft after materialize to prevent duplicate display

## Summary

- Adds end-to-end inline image rendering for TUI and WebUI when agents use image-viewing tools
- Image tool emits `MEDIA:<path>` text blocks + `{type:"image"}` base64 blocks; gateway detects markers and injects images into `chat.history` responses
- Security: path traversal check before `path.normalize()`, `\0` byte rejection, absolute-path-only guard
- Concurrency: module-level `/g` regex matches collected synchronously before any `await` I/O
- DRY: shared `MEDIA_IMAGE_LINE_RE` regex in `src/media/parse.ts` used by TUI, gateway, and WebUI

## Screenshots

**TUI (Docker terminal) + WebUI (OpenClaw Control) — inline image rendering**

![TUI and WebUI inline image rendering](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ademczuk/openclaw/feat/tui-inline-image-rendering/docs/images/tui-webui-inline-image-rendering.png)

Agent executes `sh /app/view-image.sh /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/cute-cat.jpg`. Left: TUI renders the image inline via iTerm2 graphics protocol. Right: WebUI renders the same image inline after the gateway injection pipeline reads the file and injects base64 into `chat.history`.

## Changed Files

| Area | Files | What |
|------|-------|------|
| Agent tools | `image-tool.ts`, `pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.ts` | Emit MEDIA + image blocks, bypass base64 stripping |
| Gateway | `server-chat.ts`, `chat.ts` | MEDIA bypass filter, image extraction/injection pipeline |
| TUI | `tool-execution.ts`, `tui-formatters.ts`, `tui-event-handlers.ts`, `tui-session-actions.ts` | Two-stage image render (file + base64 fallback) |
| WebUI | `app-tool-stream.ts`, `chat-event-reload.ts`, `app-gateway.ts`, `grouped-render.ts`, `tool-cards.ts` | Detect images in tool stream, reload history |
| Shared | `src/media/parse.ts` | `MEDIA_IMAGE_LINE_RE` regex constant |
| Tests | 6 test files | 118 tests covering all pipelines |

## Test Plan

```bash
pnpm vitest run src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts                    # 27 tests
pnpm vitest run src/gateway/server-methods/chat.media-injection.test.ts # 26 tests
pnpm vitest run src/tui/tui-event-handlers.test.ts                     # 15 tests
pnpm vitest run src/tui/tui-formatters.test.ts                         # 35 tests
cd ui && pnpm vitest run --config vitest.node.config.ts src/ui/app-tool-stream.node.test.ts  # 8 tests
cd ui && pnpm vitest run src/ui/chat-event-reload.test.ts              # 7 tests
pnpm tsgo   # type-check clean
pnpm check  # lint clean
```

---

Supersedes #36641 (closed for clean commit history and fresh bot review).

body:
## Summary

- **Bug**: When a user sends images via a messaging channel (Telegram, WhatsApp, Discord) and the first model turn fails (e.g. context overflow), no assistant message is generated. `pruneProcessedHistoryImages` only pruned image blocks from user messages *before the last assistant message*, so without any assistant reply the images were never evicted. Every subsequent turn re-sent the same images, causing an infinite context overflow loop that could only be escaped via `/reset`.
- **Fix**: Expand the prune boundary from `lastAssistantIndex` to `max(lastAssistantIndex, lastUserIndex)`, so stale image-bearing user turns are cleaned up even when the model never successfully responded. The latest user message's images are preserved (it may be the current turn).
- Adds a test case covering the no-assistant-reply scenario.

## Repro

1. Send 3+ large photos via Telegram to an agent using a model with limited context
2. First turn fails with "Context overflow: prompt too large for the model"
3. Send any follow-up text message
4. Observe the same images being re-processed (resized) on every subsequent turn indefinitely
5. Agent is permanently stuck until `/reset`

## Test plan

- [x] Existing `pruneProcessedHistoryImages` tests pass (3/3)
- [x] New test case: prunes images from earlier user message when a newer user message follows without assistant reply
- [x] `oxfmt --check` passes
- [x] `oxlint --type-aware` passes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: fix(agents): prune history images even without prior assistant reply

## Summary
- add a bounded cache policy to channel registry loader entries
- support an optional max entry override (default remains conservative)
- add a focused unit test proving capped cache eviction/reload behavior

## Why
The loader cache previously had no upper bound. In long-lived processes with many unique channel ids, this can cause avoidable memory growth from one-off lookups.

## Risk
Low. The change only affects lookups after the cache reaches capacity, where old entries are intentionally dropped and lazily repopulated.
title: perf: bound channel registry loader cache growth

body:
## Summary

- Problem: embedded agent runs treated plain-text completions as success even when the prompt explicitly required a tool call.
- Why it matters: after model fallback, the runner could accept replies like `HEARTBEAT_OK` or session-local summaries instead of actually performing the required tool action.
- What changed: added `requireToolUse` support to the embedded runner, plus conservative prompt-based inference for explicit `call/use the X tool` instructions and the heartbeat `Read HEARTBEAT.md` prompt.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not change general chat behavior, tool schemas, or OpenResponses `tool_choice` handling.

- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

Runs that explicitly require tool use now return an error instead of succeeding with text-only output when no required tool was called. Heartbeat-style `Read HEARTBEAT.md...` prompts now require a `read` tool call before `HEARTBEAT_OK` counts as success.

2. Return a plain-text assistant response without any tool execution.
3. Observe the run result.

### Expected

- The runner should not count the run as success when the required tool was never called.

### Actual

## Evidence

  - Heartbeat prompt inference requires `read` tool usage.

This reverts commit 874db21d

## Summary

- **Problem:** Commit 874db21d added Linuxbrew support to the Docker image, but it was lost during a later upstream rebase/merge. The brew installation block in `Dockerfile` and the corresponding `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_BREW` wiring in `docker-setup.sh` were dropped, meaning Docker builds with `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_BREW=1` would silently skip brew installation.
- **Why it matters:** Without brew in the Docker container, skill dependencies that rely on Homebrew (e.g. `uv`, `go`, `signal-cli`, 1Password CLI) cannot be installed, breaking skill functionality for Docker users (issue #14593).
- **What changed:**
  - Re-added the optional Linuxbrew installation block in `Dockerfile` (guarded by `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_BREW` build arg), including the `linuxbrew` user setup, Homebrew env vars, and PATH additions.
  - Re-added `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_BREW` env export, `.env` persistence (`upsert_env`), `--build-arg` passthrough, and `OPENCLAW_SKIP_BUILD` guard in `docker-setup.sh`.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No changes to gateway logic, runtime behavior, or non-Docker paths. The Docker CLI installation block, OCI labels, healthcheck, sandbox setup, and other recent Dockerfile additions are untouched.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature

- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #14593

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `docker build --build-arg OPENCLAW_INSTALL_BREW=1` now correctly installs Linuxbrew in the container, enabling brew-based skill installation.
- `docker-setup.sh` respects `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_BREW` env var and passes it through to the build and `.env` file.
- Added `OPENCLAW_SKIP_BUILD` guard to skip image build/pull when set (allows pre-built images).

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No` (brew install fetches from Homebrew upstream, same as the original commit)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux (Docker container, Debian bookworm base)
- Runtime/container: Docker with `node:22-bookworm`
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config: `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_BREW=1`

### Steps

1. `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_BREW=1 ./docker-setup.sh`
2. Exec into the container: `docker exec -it <container> bash`
3. Run `brew --version`

### Expected

- Brew is installed and available at `/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/brew`.

### Actual (before fix)

- Brew not found; skills requiring brew dependencies fail to install.

## Evidence

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

_(Manual Docker build verification -- see Human Verification below)_

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Built Docker image with `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_BREW=1`, confirmed `brew --version` works inside the container.
- Edge cases checked: Build without `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_BREW` (no brew installed, no errors). `OPENCLAW_SKIP_BUILD` skips build as expected.
- What you did **not** verify: Skill installation end-to-end via brew inside Docker (e.g. `uv`, `signal-cli`).

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No` (new optional env var `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_BREW`, defaults to empty/off)
- Migration needed? `No`

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert this commit, or simply don't set `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_BREW=1`.
- Files/config to restore: `Dockerfile`, `docker-setup.sh`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: Brew install hanging during Docker build (network issues), container size bloat (~500MB when enabled).

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Brew installation adds ~500MB to the Docker image when enabled.
  - Mitigation: Opt-in only via `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_BREW=1` build arg; disabled by default.
- Risk: Homebrew install script fetched from the internet at build time could change behavior.
  - Mitigation: Post-install validation checks (`brew` binary exists and is executable); build fails explicitly if install fails.
title: Fix: restore Linuxbrew installation support in Docker builds

## Summary
When memory search is configured with an embedding provider and query-time embedding fails with auth scope errors (e.g. `missing_scope: model.request`), OpenClaw currently fails the query even though FTS data is available.

This PR adds a targeted fallback:
- detect auth scope errors on query embedding
- if FTS is available, degrade that query to FTS-only results instead of throwing
- keep existing behavior for non-auth embedding failures

## Why
This preserves memory recall in OAuth scope-mismatch scenarios and avoids hard failures for users who still have local FTS index data.

## Changes
- `src/memory/manager.ts`

  - wrap `embedQueryWithTimeout()` in try/catch
  - on scope/auth error + FTS available, return keyword results
- `src/memory/manager.async-search.test.ts`
  - add regression test: falls back to FTS when embed query throws `missing_scope`

## Testing
Could not run full suite in this environment due upstream dependency install failure while preparing a git-hosted package (`@tloncorp/api` EACCES in npm cache during pnpm install).
Added focused unit regression test in-tree.
title: memory: degrade to FTS on OpenAI missing_scope embedding failures

## Summary

- **Problem:** GIF image attachments sent to vision models (Gemini, Grok, etc.) cause HTTP 400 errors because most providers only accept JPEG, PNG, or WebP.
- **Root cause:** `resizeImageBase64IfNeeded` short-circuits when images are within size/dimension limits, passing GIFs unchanged to the model API.
- **Fix:** Add a format-conversion guard so GIF images always go through the JPEG conversion path, regardless of size.
- **Scope:** 2 files, ~27 lines added. No runtime logic changes outside the GIF path.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Skills / tool execution

## Linked Issue

- Fixes #36635

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

GIF attachments from any channel (Signal, Telegram, etc.) are now auto-converted to JPEG before being sent to vision models. Users no longer see HTTP 400 errors.

## Security Impact

- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

```
pnpm vitest run src/agents/tool-images.test.ts
# Test Files  1 passed (1)
# Tests       6 passed (6)
```

New test creates a real 1×1 GIF via sharp, passes it through `sanitizeContentBlocksImages`, and asserts the output mimeType is no longer `image/gif`.

## Compatibility

- Config/env changes: No
- Migration needed: No

- No config changes needed.

## Risks and Mitigations

body:
## Summary

Fixes #36635

Most vision APIs (Google Gemini, xAI Grok, OpenAI) return HTTP 400 for `image/gif` with:
> Invalid request content: Downloaded response does not contain a valid JPG, PNG, or WebP image.

The raw error leaks into chat as the bot's reply and can jam the agent for subsequent turns.

## Root cause

`describeImageWithModel` forwarded `params.mime` and the raw buffer directly to the provider. When the inbound file was a GIF, the MIME type `image/gif` was passed unchanged, causing rejection.

## Fix

Two-file change:

1. **`src/media/image-ops.ts`** — add `convertGifFirstFrameToPng(buffer)`: loads `sharp` with `{ pages: 1 }` (first animation frame only, to keep memory flat for large animated GIFs) and emits PNG.

2. **`src/media-understanding/providers/image.ts`** — before building the base64 payload, check `imageMime === "image/gif"` and run the conversion. Falls back silently on failure so the error surfaces through the normal model-response path rather than crashing the pipeline.

## Impact

- Static and animated GIFs sent via Signal (or any other channel) now produce a description instead of an error.
- Signal's built-in GIF picker (which sends mp4) is unaffected — this only triggers on `image/gif` MIME.
- No change to PNG / JPEG / WebP / HEIC paths.

---
🤖 Generated with Claude Code
title: fix(media): convert GIF to PNG before passing to vision models

body:
updateLastRoute was bumping updatedAt on routing changes, causing idle-reset to miss stale sessions that had no real user turns.

**This was AI-assisted using Claude, but manually tested and reviewed by me (the human).**

  ## Summary

  - **Problem:** `updateLastRoute` set `updatedAt` to `Date.now()` on every routing metadata update, even though no user turn occurred. This made idle-reset think the session was still active.
  - **Why it matters:** Sessions that should have been reset after the idle window (e.g. 30 minutes) were kept alive indefinitely by background routing updates, so users got stale context instead of a fresh session.
  - **What changed:** `updateLastRoute` now uses `mergeSessionEntryPreserveActivity` for existing sessions, leaving `updatedAt` untouched so idle-reset evaluates against the real last-turn time.
  - **What did NOT change:** New sessions (no existing entry) still go through `mergeSessionEntry` as before. All other session write paths (`recordInboundSession`, `initSessionState`, etc.) are untouched.

  ## Change Type (select all)

  - [x] Bug fix
  - [ ] Feature
  - [ ] Refactor
  - [ ] Docs
  - [ ] Security hardening
  - [ ] Chore/infra

  ## Scope (select all touched areas)

  - [x] Gateway / orchestration
  - [ ] Skills / tool execution
  - [ ] Auth / tokens
  - [x] Memory / storage
  - [ ] Integrations
  - [ ] API / contracts
  - [ ] UI / DX
  - [ ] CI/CD / infra

  ## Linked Issue/PR

  - Closes #27102
  - Related #

  ## User-visible / Behavior Changes

  Sessions that have been idle past the configured `idleMinutes` threshold will now correctly reset on the next inbound message, even if `updateLastRoute` was called in between.

  ## Security Impact (required)

  - New permissions/capabilities? `No`
  - Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
  - New/changed network calls? `No`
  - Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
  - Data access scope changed? `No`

  ## Repro + Verification

  ### Environment

  - OS: macOS
  - Runtime/container: Node 22+ / Bun
  - Model/provider: N/A (session store logic)
  - Integration/channel (if any): Any (repro uses WhatsApp)
  - Relevant config (redacted): `session.reset: { mode: "daily", atHour: 4, idleMinutes: 30 }`

  ### Steps

  1. Have an existing session that has been idle for longer than `idleMinutes`.
  2. Trigger a routing update via `updateLastRoute` (e.g. inbound message arrives, `recordInboundSession` calls `updateLastRoute` before `initSessionState`).
  3. `initSessionState` evaluates idle reset.

  ### Expected

  - Session resets because the last real turn was beyond the idle window.

  ### Actual (before fix)

  - Session was kept alive because `updateLastRoute` had bumped `updatedAt` to now.

  ## Evidence

  - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
  - [ ] Trace/log snippets
  - [ ] Screenshot/recording
  - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

  Two new tests added:
  - `session.test.ts`: "stale session resets after updateLastRoute (idle)" — confirms `initSessionState` still resets an idle session even after `updateLastRoute` runs.
  - `store.session-key-normalization.test.ts`: "updateLastRoute preserves updatedAt for existing sessions" — confirms `updatedAt` is not overwritten by routing updates.

  ## Human Verification (required)

  - Verified scenarios: Both new tests pass; existing test suite passes (`pnpm test`).
  - Edge cases checked: New session (no existing entry) still gets a proper `updatedAt` via the default `mergeSessionEntry` path.
  - What you did **not** verify: Live multi-channel routing on a running gateway instance.

  ## Compatibility / Migration

  - Backward compatible? `Yes`
  - Config/env changes? `No`
  - Migration needed? `No`

  ## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

  - How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert the single commit; the change is isolated to `updateLastRoute` in
  `src/config/sessions/store.ts`.
  - Files/config to restore: `src/config/sessions/store.ts`
  - Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: Sessions resetting too aggressively (i.e. routing updates no longer keeping sessions alive when they should — though by design they should not).

  ## Risks and Mitigations

  - **Risk:** Edge case where `mergeSessionEntryPreserveActivity` behaves differently from `mergeSessionEntry` for fields beyond `updatedAt`.
    - **Mitigation:** The only difference is timestamp preservation; both functions share the same merge logic otherwise. New test explicitly validates `updatedAt` is preserved.
